questions.text: What does 'Al-Furqan' mean?
questions.answer: The Criterion (the standard to judge between right and wrong).

questions.text: What was the disbelievers' criticism of the Quran being revealed gradually?
questions.answer: They asked: 'Why was the Quran not sent down to him all at once?'

questions.text: What was Allah's reason for revealing the Quran in stages?
questions.answer: To strengthen the heart of the Prophet (SAW) and to explain it in a better way.

questions.text: How did the disbelievers mock the human nature of the Prophet (SAW)?
questions.answer: They said: 'What is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets?'

questions.text: What will the wrongdoer do on the Day of Judgment out of regret (Verse 27)?
questions.answer: He will bite on his hands, saying: 'Oh, I wish I had taken a path with the Messenger'.

questions.text: What is the regret regarding 'friendship' mentioned in Verse 28?
questions.answer: 'Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend'.

questions.text: What complaint will the Messenger make against his people on the Last Day (Verse 30)?
questions.answer: 'O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Quran as something abandoned (Mahjura)'.

questions.text: Who are the 'Ibadur Rahman'?
questions.answer: The 'Servants of the Most Merciful' described at the end of the Surah.

questions.text: How do the 'Ibadur Rahman' respond when the ignorant address them?
questions.answer: They say: 'Salam' (peace).

questions.text: What is the prayer of the 'Ibadur Rahman' regarding Hell?
questions.answer: 'Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering'.

questions.text: What is their attitude towards spending (Verse 67)?
questions.answer: They are neither extravagant nor stingy, but maintain a balance between the two.

questions.text: Which three major sins are mentioned in Verse 68 that they avoid?
questions.answer: Shirk (associating partners with Allah), killing an innocent soul, and adultery/fornication.

questions.text: What happens to the bad deeds of those who repent, believe, and do righteous work?
questions.answer: Allah replaces their evil deeds with good ones.

questions.text: What is the prayer of the 'Ibadur Rahman' for their families?
questions.answer: 'Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us an example for the righteous'.

questions.text: What is the 'barrier' between the two seas mentioned in Verse 53?
questions.answer: An invisible partition (Barzakh) and a forbidden separation between fresh water and salty, bitter water.

questions.text: From what did Allah create every human being in this Surah?
questions.answer: From water, then made for him blood relationship and relationship by marriage.

questions.text: What is the shadow's purpose mentioned in Verse 45?
questions.answer: It is a sign of Allah's power; He could have made it still, but He made the sun its guide.

questions.text: What is the 'Gharama' mentioned in Verse 65?
questions.answer: The punishment of Hell which is 'continuous' or 'inescapable'.

questions.text: Why does Allah say He wouldn't care for people if not for their 'Dua' (Verse 77)?
questions.answer: Because worship and calling upon Him is the primary purpose of their existence; without it, they have no value to Him.

questions.text: How do the servants of the Merciful spend their nights?
questions.answer: Prostrating and standing in prayer before their Lord.
